notes: Lodi dynasty was founded by Bahlol Lodi, the last ruling dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate, ruling from 1451 to 1526. In AD 1476, Bahlol Lodi defeated the Sultan of Jaunpur and annexed it to the Delhi Sultanate. He also brought the ruler of Kalpi and Dholpur under the Suzerainty of Delhi.

Ibrahim Lodi was the last Sultan of the Delhi Sultanate (1206–1526), he became Sultan in 1517 after the death of his father Sikandar Khan Lodi. In 1526, he was defeated and killed in the Battle of Panipat by Babur, giving way to the emergence of the Mughal Empire in India.

Lodi dynasty (1451 - 1526). It was founded by Bahlul Lodi (1451 to 1489 AD). Sultan Sikandar Lodi founded Agra in 1504. Famous Historic City and their Founder: Fatehpur Sikri (Uttar Pradesh) - Akbar, Hampi (Karnataka) - Harihara I and Bukka Raya I, Udaipur (Rajasthan) - Udai Singh II, Warangal (Telangana) - Kakatiya dynasty. Q.342. Which of the following mosques/masjids was built during the reign of Lodi dynasty of the Delhi Sultanate? SSC CPO 03/10/2023 (Evening) (a) Begumpuri mosque, Delhi (b) Quwwat al-Islam, Delhi (c) Lal Masjid, Delhi (d) Moth ki Masjid, Delhi Sol.342.(d) Moth ki Masjid, Delhi. It was built in 1505 by Wazir Miya Bhoiya. Begumpuri mosque (Delhi) - It was constructed by Muhammad Tughluq . Q.343. “Double Dome” feature of Indo-Islamic architecture was introduced by which dynasty of Delhi Sultanate? SSC MTS 20/06/2023 (Morning) (a) Slave Dynasty (b) Khilji Dynasty (c) Tughlaq Dynasty (d) Lodi Dynasty Sol.343.(d) Lodi Dynasty (1451-1526). Double dome - Built of two layers. There is one layer inside which provides a ceiling to the interior of the building. The other layer is the outer one which crowns the buildings. Example - Humayun's Tomb.
Nizam Khan {assumed the title Sikandar Shah,founded Agra in 1504, relocated the capital from Delhi to Agra, pen name Gulrukh}.

The Jaunpur sultanate was ruled by the sharqi dynasty. It was founded by Khwajah-i-Jahan Malik Sarwar in 1394. In 1479, Sultan Hussain Khan was defeated by the forces of Afghan chieftain Bahlul Lodhi and this dynasty came to an end. Mughal Period Q.346. Consider the following statements in the context of the Mughal Rule. 1. The Mansabdari System was introduced by Akbar in 1571. 2. The Second Battle of Panipat was fought in 1556. 3. Maharana Pratap Singh of Mewar was a contemporary of Babur. Which of the statements given above is/are correct? SSC MTS 28/10/2024 (2nd Shift) (a) Only 2 (b) Both 1 and 2 (c) Both 2 and 3 (d) 1, 2 and 3 Sol.346.(b) Both 1 and 2. Maharana Pratap Singh was a contemporary of the third Mughal emperor Akbar. He is notable for leading the Rajput resistance against Akbar's expansionist policies, including in the Battle of Haldighati (1576) and the Battle of Dewair (1582). The Second Battle of Panipat was fought between Akbar and Hemu. The mansabdari system was basically a grading system in order to rank the officers based on their ranks and salaries.
Swami Haridasa. The famous musician Tansen was one of the Navratnas (nine gems) in the court of Emperor Akbar, who bestowed the title 'Mian' upon him.

Mir Saman. Mir Bahri (mir-i-bahr) - Responsible for the maintenance of the river and seaports. Mir Bakshi - Headed military department, nobility, information and intelligence agencies. Mir-e-aatish - Head of the ordinance department.

Jean-Baptiste Tavernier. He came to India during the reign of Shah Jahan. Antonio Monserrate : A Spanish missionary, came during the reign of Akbar. Seydi Ali Reis : He was a Turkish traveler who visited India in the 16th century. Peter Mundy : A European philosopher and foreign traveler came to India during the reign of Shah Jahan.
Parwez Shah (Son of Mughal Emperor Jahangir) built “Pathar Ki Masjid” on the banks of River Ganga, Patna (Bihar) in 1621.

On 12 August 1765, the Mughal emperor (Shah Alam II) appointed the East India Company as the Diwan of Bengal. Q.352. _______ in Gujarat was the emporium of western trade during the Mughal Period. SSC MTS 20/07/2022 (Evening) (a) Navasari (b) Narmada (c) Porbandar (d) Surat Sol.352.(d) Surat. It grew famous for all types of textiles, particularly cotton textiles.

Persian. Mughal chronicles such as the Akbar Nama were written in Persian. Some of the famous Books during Mughal Reign are Humayun Nama was written by Gulbadan Begum, Tuzuk - E Babari - Babur, Akbar Nama- Abul Fazl, Tarikh E Akbari - Abul Fazl, Tahakik E Hind- Albiruni, Laila Majnu- Amir Khasuru, Shahnamah- Firdousi etc Q.354. With reference to the Land Revenue System of Mughal Empire, the term ' was the amount assessed. SSC CPO 09/11/2022 (Morning) (a) Banjar (b) Polaj (c) Hasil (d) Jama Sol.354.(d) Jama. The arrangements for collection of land revenue consisted of two stages : first assessment and then actual collection. The Jama was the amount assessed whereas the Hasil was the amount received or collected. Banjar was the worst kind of land that was left out of cultivation for five years or upwards.

Dara Shikoh. He was the eldest son and heir-apparent of the Mughal emperor Shah Jahan. He completed the translation of fifty upanishads from their original Sanskrit to Persian in 1657 so that they could be studied by muslim scholars.
Aziz-ud-din Alamgir II. Alamgir II (Reign - 1754-1759) was the fifteenth Mughal Emperor.
